# UC-10.12.39 · FedRAMP Continuous Monitoring

## Description

Automates ConMon evidence: vulnerability scanning, logging, and boundary defenses for FedRAMP Moderate/High.

## Value

Automates ConMon evidence: vulnerability scanning, logging, and boundary defenses for FedRAMP Moderate/High.

## Implementation

Map controls to saved searches; export to POA&M workflow; 3PAO evidence packs from scheduled PDFs.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: SCAP scanners, cloud TAs (AWS/Azure/GCP).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"`, `sourcetype="tenable:scan"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map controls to saved searches; export to POA&M workflow; 3PAO evidence packs from scheduled PDFs.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=govcloud (sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" OR sourcetype="tenable:scan")
| search (eventName="DeleteTrail" OR severity="CRITICAL") AND tag=fedramp_boundary
| stats latest(_time) as last_event by resource_id, control_id
| where last_event < relative_time(now(),"-30d@d")
```

Understanding this SPL

**FedRAMP Continuous Monitoring** — Automates ConMon evidence: vulnerability scanning, logging, and boundary defenses for FedRAMP Moderate/High.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"`, `sourcetype="tenable:scan"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SCAP scanners, cloud TAs (AWS/Azure/GCP). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: govcloud; **sourcetype**: aws:cloudtrail, tenable:scan. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=govcloud, sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by resource_id, control_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where last_event < relative_time(now(),"-30d@d")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gantt (control testing), Table (stale evidence), Scorecard (ConMon % green).

## SPL

```spl
index=govcloud (sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" OR sourcetype="tenable:scan")
| search (eventName="DeleteTrail" OR severity="CRITICAL") AND tag=fedramp_boundary
| stats latest(_time) as last_event by resource_id, control_id
| where last_event < relative_time(now(),"-30d@d")
```

## Visualization

Gantt (control testing), Table (stale evidence), Scorecard (ConMon % green).
